title: Microsoft GraphRAG Python API: Index, Query, and Search
description: Working code for Microsoft GraphRAG's Python API showing how to programmatically build indexes, run global/local/DRIFT searches, and generate domain-specific prompts. Covers the full workflow from configuration to entity extraction, community detection, and multi-level querying. Uses the async API for production deployments.
code:
from graphrag.api import build_index, global_search, local_search, drift_search
from graphrag.config.models.graph_rag_config import GraphRagConfig

# 1. Load configuration
config = GraphRagConfig.from_file("./my-project/settings.yaml")

# 2. Build index (chunks docs, extracts entities/relationships,
#    builds knowledge graph, detects communities, generates summaries)
results = await build_index(config=config, verbose=True)

# 3. Global Search - holistic, dataset-wide questions
response, context = await global_search(
    config=config,
    entities=results.entities,
    communities=results.communities,
    community_reports=results.community_reports,
    community_level=2,
    query="What are the main themes across all documents?",
    response_type="Multiple Paragraphs"
)

# 4. Local Search - entity-focused questions
response, context = await local_search(
    config=config,
    entities=results.entities,
    text_units=results.text_units,
    relationships=results.relationships,
    query="Tell me about the relationship between X and Y"
)

# 5. DRIFT Search - multi-level reasoning
response, context = await drift_search(
    config=config,
    entities=results.entities,
    query="How do different themes relate across the corpus?"
)

print(response)

title: Multi-Granularity Chunking with Parent-Child Retrieval
description: Chunking and retrieval pattern that creates parent chunks (large, e.g. 2000 tokens) and child chunks (small, e.g. 500 tokens) at multiple granularities. Retrieves using small child chunks for precision but returns the parent chunk for full context. Score of a parent chunk equals the max score of its child chunks. LGMGC implements this with LLM logits-guided boundaries, achieving best performance and smallest variance across chunk size hyperparameters.

title: Multimodal RAG Survey: Scaling Beyond Context for Document Understanding
description: Comprehensive survey of 45+ multimodal RAG methods for document understanding covering text, tables, charts, and images. Organizes approaches by domain (open vs. closed), retrieval modality (image-only vs. image+text), granularity (page-level vs. element-level), and hybrid enhancements (graph-based, agent-based). Key trends: shift from page-level to element-level retrieval, multimodal knowledge graphs (mKG-RAG), and multi-agent workflows (ViDoRAG, HM-RAG).

title: Neo4j GraphRAG Python Library
description: Official Neo4j GraphRAG Python package (1.1K+ GitHub stars) providing SimpleKGPipeline for knowledge graph construction, multiple retrievers (VectorRetriever, VectorCypherRetriever, Text2CypherRetriever, Hybrid retrievers), and a GraphRAG class for end-to-end Q&A. Supports multiple LLM backends (OpenAI, Anthropic, Ollama, Google, Cohere, Mistral, Bedrock) and vector DB integrations (Weaviate, Pinecone, Qdrant). Extensible TextSplitter base class for custom chunking.

title: OpenAI Embeddings (text-embedding-3-small/large)
description: OpenAI's embedding models: text-embedding-3-small ($0.02/1M tokens, 1536 dims) is the cheapest managed option and best default for new projects. text-embedding-3-large ($0.13/1M tokens, 3072 dims) offers higher quality at 6.5x cost. Both support Matryoshka dimension reduction (truncate to 256-1536 dims without re-embedding). No update in over 2 years; weaker multilingual performance vs. dedicated models; 8191-token context limit.

title: Production RAG Cost Optimization Playbook
description: Systematic cost optimization playbook achieving 80-90% cost reduction for production RAG systems. Key strategies: deduplication (40-70% embedding savings), batch processing (50% API cost savings), float8 quantization (75% storage reduction with less than 0.3% accuracy loss), caching (60-80% hit rates reducing latency from 150ms to less than 20ms), context-aware chunking (80-85% token usage reduction), and conditional reranking (skip for high-confidence queries, 40-60% fewer invocations). Total cost of ownership for 10M documents drops from $482/month to $187/month.

title: Production RAG Monitoring: Per-Stage Latency Breakdown and SLOs
description: Pattern for monitoring production RAG systems with per-stage latency breakdowns and SLO definitions. Target p95 retrieval less than 200ms, p95 LLM generation less than 3s. LLM inference dominates the latency budget (~2.5s of ~2.7s total). Use OpenTelemetry plus Prometheus plus Grafana for end-to-end tracing. Key signals: per-stage latency, token counts, cache hit rates, 'I don't know' rate, answer length distribution. Use head-based sampling (1/1000) for high-volume embedding calls and tail-based sampling for expensive LLM inference.

title: Pseudo-Instruction Chunking (PIC) Pattern
description: Chunking pattern that uses document summaries as pseudo-instructions to guide chunk boundaries. Without knowing real user queries in advance, summaries serve as proxies for the types of questions users might ask. Groups sentences whose embeddings are similar to the summary embedding into coherent chunks. Achieves highest average retrieval performance across 6 benchmarks. Validates that aligning chunks with query intent significantly improves retrieval.

title: Qdrant Vector Database
description: High-performance open-source vector database written in Rust, supporting dense vectors, sparse vectors (SPLADE/BM25), and multi-vector late interaction (ColBERT). Offers the best price-to-performance ratio among vector databases, with advanced payload filtering, quantization, and a flexible Query API for hybrid search with RRF and DBSF fusion.

title: R3-RAG: Reinforcement Learning for Step-by-Step Retrieval
description: Agentic RAG pattern using RL to train LLMs to iteratively reason and retrieve step-by-step. Combines outcome rewards (answer correctness) and process rewards (relevance-based document verification at each retrieval step). Uses PPO training with cold start from SFT on synthesized interleaved reasoning-retrieval trajectories. Outperforms IRCoT by approximately 15 percentage points on multi-hop QA. Uses 22.8% fewer tokens than ReSearch while achieving higher accuracy. Strong transferability across retrievers (BM25, E5, BGE) with less than 3% variation.

title: RAG Data Versioning and Regression Testing
description: Production pattern for versioning document stores and embedding models alongside RAG pipelines. Uses lakeFS for data versioning, stores embedding commit hashes alongside vectors, logs model names/versions with every request, and periodically re-runs fixed evaluation sets to catch regressions. Key principle: treat your vector store like application code, with version control, rollback capability, and CI/CD testing. Ensures that model upgrades, re-chunking, or re-embedding don't silently degrade retrieval quality.

title: RAG in Production with Haystack
description: O'Reilly published practical blueprint (132 pages, April 2025) for building RAG applications using the open-source Haystack framework. Covers extractive and generative QA, semantic search, and building secure ChatGPT-like experiences on technical documentation. Provides LLM software development lifecycle mental models for production RAG systems.

title: RAG-Critic: Automated Error Identification and Correction in RAG
description: ACL 2025 paper proposing a hierarchical RAG error taxonomy (3 tiers, 4000+ error types) and a critic-guided agentic workflow. The Generate-Critic-Plan-Execute loop identifies errors, selects from 5 action functions (Retrieval, Rewrite, Decompose, Refine, Generate), and corrects them. A 3B critic model outperforms 70B models on fine-grained error identification (58.3% vs 31.5%). Achieves +2.8% to +5.3% F1 improvement over Self-RAG, FLARE, and MetaRAG baselines.

title: RAGAS Evaluation Framework
description: The most popular RAG evaluation framework (13.3K GitHub stars) for automated quality assessment. v0.3+ introduces DiscreteMetric for pass/fail scoring, @experiment() decorator for evaluation loops, and llm_factory() for easy LLM setup. Core metrics: Faithfulness, FactualCorrectness, ResponseRelevancy, LLMContextRecall, ContextPrecision, NoiseSensitivity. Uses dual-judge averaging for more granular scoring. Supports OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Ollama.

title: Recursive Semantic Chunking (RSC) Pattern
description: Advanced chunking pattern that recursively splits large chunks and merges small ones to maintain semantic coherence. Starts with initial semantic chunking in embedding space, then recursively splits chunks exceeding a threshold (T_chunk=1500 chars) with decreasing delta, merges short chunks (below T_merge=350 chars) with their most similar neighbor, and applies final size adjustment. Outperforms fixed and basic semantic chunking on contextual relevancy metrics.

title: SIM-RAG: Self-Practicing When to Stop Retrieving
description: Agentic RAG pattern addressing the critical challenge of when to stop retrieving in multi-round RAG. Trains a lightweight Critic (783M or 2.85B Flan-T5) to assess information sufficiency using self-practiced synthetic data. Even the lightweight SIM-RAGlite outperforms Self-RAG on HotpotQA (27.8% vs 17.1% EM) with 1/10th the parameters. Key finding: a specialized lightweight Critic outperforms GPT-4-as-Critic for complex multi-hop tasks. Avoids both over-confidence and over-retrieval.

title: SPLADE Sparse Retrieval with Sentence Transformers
description: Working code for SPLADE sparse neural retrieval using the Sentence Transformers SparseEncoder API. Demonstrates encoding a corpus with SPLADE, performing semantic search, and inspecting which tokens influenced each result. SPLADE produces interpretable sparse representations where each dimension corresponds to a vocabulary term with an importance weight.
code:
from sentence_transformers import SparseEncoder
from sentence_transformers.util import semantic_search

# 1. Load SPLADE model
model = SparseEncoder("naver/splade-v3-distilbert")

# 2. Encode corpus
corpus = [
    "Retrieval-Augmented Generation combines information retrieval with text generation.",
    "Vector databases store high-dimensional embeddings for similarity search.",
    "Hybrid search combines keyword and semantic matching for better recall.",
    "Chunking strategies determine how documents are split for embedding.",
    "Reranking improves retrieval precision by scoring candidates with a cross-encoder.",
]
corpus_embeddings = model.encode(corpus, convert_to_tensor=True)

# 3. Query and search
queries = ["How does RAG work?"]
query_embeddings = model.encode(queries, convert_to_tensor=True)

results = semantic_search(
    query_embeddings,
    corpus_embeddings,
    top_k=3,
    score_function=model.similarity
)

# 4. Inspect influential tokens per result
for query_id, query in enumerate(queries):
    pointwise_scores = model.intersection(
        query_embeddings[query_id], corpus_embeddings
    )
    for res in results[query_id]:
        corpus_id = res["corpus_id"]
        score = res["score"]
        token_scores = model.decode(pointwise_scores[corpus_id], top_k=10)
        print(f"Score: {score:.4f} - {corpus[corpus_id][:60]}...")
        print(f"  Top tokens: {token_scores}")

title: Separate Retrieval and Generation Scoring
description: Critical production pattern: score retrieval and generation quality separately, never mix them into a single 'answer quality' metric. Retrieval metrics (context precision, context recall, context relevance) measure whether the right information was found. Generation metrics (faithfulness, answer relevance) measure whether the LLM used that information correctly. A RAG system can score 0.95 faithfulness and still produce wrong answers if retrieved content is stale or incorrect.

title: Set Up Qdrant Hybrid Search with Dense and Sparse Vectors
description: Complete workflow for implementing hybrid search in Qdrant combining dense vector semantic search with sparse vector keyword matching (BM25/SPLADE), fused using Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF). This pattern significantly outperforms either dense or sparse search alone, especially for queries containing specific terms or IDs.
workflow: Install: pip install qdrant-client[fastembed] fastembed
Create a Qdrant collection with both dense and sparse vector configurations
Configure dense vectors (e.g., all-MiniLM-L6-v2, 384 dimensions, cosine distance)
Configure sparse vectors with SparseVectorParams for BM25 or SPLADE
Index documents using client.upsert() with both dense and sparse vector representations
For search, use client.query_points() with two Prefetch queries (one dense, one sparse)
Apply FusionQuery(fusion=models.Fusion.RRF) to merge results from both prefetched queries
Optionally add ColBERT late-interaction reranking as a third stage for maximum precision
Monitor recall@k and latency metrics; tune the k limits on each prefetch stage

title: UniversalRAG: Modality-Aware Routing for Multimodal Retrieval
description: Multimodal RAG pattern that dynamically identifies the most appropriate modality-specific corpus (text, image, table, video) and performs targeted retrieval. Key finding: forcing all modalities into a unified embedding space causes a modality gap where retrieval favors items of the same modality as the query. Routing to modality-specific corpora avoids this gap. Supports both training-based routers (Qwen3-VL-2B, InternVL3.5-1B) and training-free routers (GPT-5). Multi-granularity retrieval (paragraphs vs. documents, clips vs. full videos) consistently improves performance.

title: Vector Database Selection Decision Framework
description: Decision framework for choosing the right vector database based on scale, operational requirements, and use case. Prototyping: ChromaDB. Production under 1M vectors without DevOps: Pinecone. Cost-sensitive production with 10-100M vectors: Qdrant. Enterprise hybrid search: Weaviate. Billions of vectors with K8s: Milvus. Existing PostgreSQL: pgvector. Migration path from dev to production scale.

Excels at enterprise RAG workloads requiring keyword and semantic search together. Available as self-hosted or managed Weaviate Cloud.

title: mRAG Design Space for Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: First systematic dissection of the multimodal RAG pipeline for large vision-language models. Investigates retrieval phase configurations (image-only, image+text, image+caption, caption-only), reranking strategies (pointwise, pairwise, listwise), and generation integration. Key findings: EVA-CLIP is the most robust zero-shot retriever; listwise reranking with LVLMs is the best zero-shot strategy (+2.6% Recall@1); adding more documents does NOT improve generation accuracy; an agentic framework with self-reflection boosts response accuracy by +5%/+2% on E-VQA/InfoSeek.

title: ChromaDB Quick Start for RAG
description: Python code for setting up ChromaDB as a local vector store for RAG applications.
code:
import chromadb
from chromadb.utils import embedding_functions

client = chromadb.PersistentClient(path="./chroma_db")
embed_fn = embedding_functions.SentenceTransformerEmbeddingFunction(
    model_name="all-MiniLM-L6-v2"
)
collection = client.get_or_create_collection(
    name="documents",
    embedding_function=embed_fn,
    metadata={"hnsw:space": "cosine"}
)

collection.add(
    documents=["RAG combines retrieval with generation", "Vector search enables semantic matching"],
    ids=["doc1", "doc2"],
    metadatas=[{"source": "blog", "topic": "rag"}, {"source": "docs", "topic": "vectors"}]
)

results = collection.query(
    query_texts=["How does RAG work?"],
    n_results=2,
    where={"topic": "rag"}
)

title: FAISS Index for Local Vector Search
description: Python code for building and querying a FAISS index for fast local similarity search.
code:
import faiss
import numpy as np
from sentence_transformers import SentenceTransformer

model = SentenceTransformer('all-MiniLM-L6-v2')

documents = [
    'RAG combines retrieval with generation',
    'Vector databases enable semantic search',
    'LoRA reduces fine-tuning memory by 90%',
    'MCP connects AI to external tools and data'
]

# Build FAISS index
embeddings = model.encode(documents)
dimension = embeddings.shape[1]
index = faiss.IndexFlatIP(dimension)
faiss.normalize_L2(embeddings)
index.add(embeddings.astype('float32'))

# Search
query = model.encode(['How does RAG work?'])
faiss.normalize_L2(query)
scores, indices = index.search(query.astype('float32'), k=2)
for i, idx in enumerate(indices[0]):
    print(f'{scores[0][i]:.3f}: {documents[idx]}')

title: Milvus Vector Search Pipeline
description: Python code for setting up Milvus with PyMilvus for production vector search.
code:
from pymilvus import MilvusClient, DataType

client = MilvusClient("http://127.0.0.1:19530")

schema = client.create_schema(auto_id=True, enable_dynamic_field=True)
schema.add_field("id", DataType.INT64, is_primary=True)
schema.add_field("vector", DataType.FLOAT_VECTOR, dim=384)
schema.add_field("text", DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=65535)
schema.add_field("source", DataType.VARCHAR, max_length=256)

index_params = client.prepare_index_params()
index_params.add_index(
    field_name="vector",
    index_type="HNSW",
    metric_type="COSINE",
    params={"M": 16, "efConstruction": 256}
)

client.create_collection(
    collection_name="documents",
    schema=schema,
    index_params=index_params
)

code:
from pinecone import Pinecone, ServerlessSpec

pc = Pinecone(api_key="your-api-key")

index_name = "documents"
if index_name not in pc.list_indexes().names():
    pc.create_index(
        name=index_name,
        dimension=384,
        metric="cosine",
        spec=ServerlessSpec(cloud="aws", region="us-east-1")
    )

index = pc.Index(index_name)

vectors = [
    {"id": "doc1", "values": [0.1] * 384, "metadata": {"text": "RAG combines retrieval with generation"}},
    {"id": "doc2", "values": [0.2] * 384, "metadata": {"text": "Vector databases enable semantic search"}}
]
index.upsert(vectors=vectors)

results = index.query(
    vector=[0.15] * 384,
    top_k=5,
    include_metadata=True

title: Weaviate Hybrid Search Setup
description: Python code for setting up Weaviate with hybrid (sparse + dense) search.
code:
import weaviate
from weaviate.classes.config import Configure

client = weaviate.connect_to_local()

collection = client.collections.create(
    name="Documents",
    vectorizer_config=Configure.Vectorizer.text2vec_transformers(),
    vector_index_config=Configure.VectorIndex.hnsw(
        distance_metric=Configure.VectorIndex.hnsw.distancemetric.COSINE
    )
)

collection.data.insert({"text": "RAG combines retrieval with generation"})
collection.data.insert({"text": "Vector databases enable semantic search"})

results = collection.query.hybrid(
    query="How does RAG work?",
    alpha=0.5,
    limit=5
)
for obj in results.objects:
    print(obj.properties["text"])
